construct.label: Somatic and ambient AI anxiety
construct.definition: Anxiety expressed as sleep disruption, bodily vigilance, background dread, avoidance, or low-specificity physiological arousal after exposure to AI capability news or demonstrations.
construct.subconstruct: bodily vigilance after a major AI model release

scenario_text: Imagine that a major AI company releases a model described by independent reviewers as noticeably better at planning, persuasion, coding, and scientific problem solving. You read the announcement during an ordinary workday. For the next several hours, nothing concrete changes around you, but you keep checking your pulse, breathing, and muscle tension because your body feels on alert.

response_scale.prompt: How anxious would this scenario make you in terms of bodily vigilance or feeling physically on alert after an AI model release?

scoring.missing_response_rule: Treat skipped responses as missing at the item level. Do not impute item scores for development, validation, or scoring analyses.
exclusion_criteria: Exclude respondents who fail the survey-level attention check specified in the preregistered study protocol.
Exclude respondents who complete the scenario and response in less than one-third of the wave-specific median reading time.
Exclude respondents who report that they could not understand the scenario after reading it.
Exclude respondents who attribute the described bodily vigilance entirely to a concurrent non-AI physical threat presented elsewhere in the survey.
Exclude respondents younger than the minimum eligible age for the study population.

interpretation_bands.label: low
interpretation_bands.interpretation: Respondent reports little or no bodily vigilance after learning about a major AI model release.

interpretation_bands.label: moderate
interpretation_bands.interpretation: Respondent reports noticeable bodily monitoring or alertness without endorsing a severe somatic anxiety response.

interpretation_bands.label: high
interpretation_bands.interpretation: Respondent reports strong physical vigilance or on-alert arousal after exposure to a major AI model release.

population.target_population: Adults in the general population, with planned subgroup analysis by baseline anxiety history, bodily vigilance sensitivity, AI news exposure, and occupational proximity to AI systems.
population.eligible_respondents: Respondents aged 18 or older who can complete the survey language without assistance and provide informed consent.
exclusion_criteria: Current inability to provide informed consent.
Duplicate panel participation within the same measurement wave.
Self-reported inability to read the survey language.

administration_mode.instructions: Present the scenario text exactly as written, followed immediately by the response prompt and the five ordered response options.
administration_mode.randomization: Randomize order within the somatic and ambient development pool while preserving the ordered response scale.
administration_mode.longitudinal_use: Use identical wording, response anchors, scoring, and eligibility rules across waves unless a new item version is released.

ethics_flags.data_protection_notes: Do not collect medical diagnoses, medication details, clinical records, panic history, or identifiable mental-health treatment history for this item.
ethics_flags.irb_notes: The item references mild bodily vigilance and should include skip rights. It is not a clinical diagnostic item and must not be interpreted as measuring panic, illness anxiety, or an anxiety disorder.
